title: Accessible Content Models, Landmarks, Headings, Links, Media, and Language · Program Brief: Accessibility Evidence
summary: Templates emit multiple mains, heading levels follow visual size, skip links target missing IDs, repeated links have vague labels, code lacks context, and media alternatives are never reviewed. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the accessibility evidence increment in WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.
Give code blocks language and context, preserve keyboard scrolling, label tables with correct header relationships, and offer text alternatives for visual diagrams.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Accessibility Evidence · Theory Accessibility Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 6575 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

An automated accessibility scanner can certify the whole static site. That claim fails the predict accessibility evidence boundary for Accessibility Evidence.

For predict accessibility evidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-accessibility-evidence — Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-accessibility-semantics-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-accessibility-semantics-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_accessibility_semantics_4(pages: int = 8, checked_pages: int = 8, structural_findings: int = 0, media_decisions_missing: int = 0, human_tasks_passed: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or checked_pages < 0:
        return False, checked_pages, "page-count"
    if checked_pages != pages:
        return False, checked_pages, "accessibility-coverage"
    if structural_findings > 0 or media_decisions_missing > 0:
        return False, structural_findings + media_decisions_missing, "accessibility-finding"
    if human_tasks_passed < 3:
        return False, human_tasks_passed, "human-evidence"
    assert checked_pages == pages
    return True, human_tasks_passed, "accessible-content-model"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Accessibility Evidence · Theory Accessibility Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-accessibility-evidence-ck8ujs" that uses case 7955 to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Accessibility Evidence · Theory Accessibility Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Accessibility Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Accessibility Evidence · Theory Accessibility Evidence, keep Accessibility Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-accessibility-evidence-ck8ujs
# Competency: ssg-accessibility-evidence.
# Case 7955: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_accessibility_evidence_ck8ujs(pages: int = 8, checked_pages: int = 8, structural_findings: int = 0, media_decisions_missing: int = 0, human_tasks_passed: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ck8ujs = "ck8ujs-sju8kc-7955"
    assert evidence_variant_ck8ujs
    evidence_axes_ck8ujs = (
        "readerck8ujs",
        "source7955",
        "parsesju8kc",
        "routeck8ujs7955",
        "artifact7955ck8ujs",
        "recoverysju8kc7955",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ck8ujs)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or checked_pages < 0:
        return False, checked_pages, "page-count"
    if checked_pages != pages:
        return False, checked_pages, "accessibility-coverage"
    if structural_findings > 0 or media_decisions_missing > 0:
        return False, structural_findings + media_decisions_missing, "accessibility-finding"
    if human_tasks_passed < 3:
        return False, human_tasks_passed, "human-evidence"
    assert checked_pages == pages
    return True, human_tasks_passed, "accessible-content-model"

For Static-site publishing and Python Build Accessibility Evidence · Theory Accessibility Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: An automated accessibility scanner can certify the whole static site.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Accessibility Evidence model · Theory Accessibility Ev…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Accessibility Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 9256 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Accessibility Evidence model · Theory Accessibility Ev….
steps.why: Retrieving Accessibility Evidence through the concrete WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Accessibility Evidence model · Theory Accessibility Ev…, keep Accessibility Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.
